Title: 9905--Building 100 Signage Refresh, 640-A69146 04/01/2026 - 03/31/2027
SetASide: Service-Disabled Veteran-Owned Small Business Set Aside
Awardee: FIRST VANGUARD RENTALS & SALES, INC. FAIRFIELD 94533
Description: FACTOR 1 TECHNICAL FACTOR 2 DELIVERY FACTOR 3 PRICE FACTOR 1 Technical Quoter shall provide a description of the supplies to be quoted. The supplies shall be in accordance with the schedules and attachments of the solicitation. The following information is required: Descriptive literature to include warranties, special features, and characteristics, etc. The Government will rate more favorably quotes that meet or exceed the requirements of the equipment and service. The Government will rate less favorably quotes that do not give enough of a description of the supplies/equipment for the Government to determine if the equipment and services meets the requirements in the statement of work. FACTOR 2 Delivery Time Ability to be flexible with final installation dates is required as installation is based on construction completion. Ability to provide any updates and deliver upon statement of work objectives in a timely manner should be clearly outlined in your response. FACTOR 3 Price The quote should contain the quoter s best terms from a price standpoint. The Government will evaluate quotes for award purposes by adding the total price for all contract line-items. Complete all highlighted columns for each contract line-item numbers (CLIN) listed in the attached spreadsheet titled Price Sheet in Section D of the solicitation. Vendor may also submit price quote on their own formatted document, but the Price Sheet SHALL be completed and included with quote submission. Price quote must be submitted separately from the other factor submissions. In accordance with FAR 8.405-2(d), the quotes will undergo a comparative evaluation to determine which quote is the best overall offer to the government in terms of high technical capability, while also providing a competitive price. Quotes will be evaluated by performing a direct comparison of one offer with another in a uniform manner to determine which quote provides the government with its needs, as identified in the RFQ. The government reserves the right to consider a response that offers more than the minimum and select that response if it provides a benefit to the government. The government has the discretion to accept other than the lowest priced offeror that provides additional benefits. The government reserves the right to select a response that provides benefit to the Government that exceeds the minimum but is not required to do so. Responses may exceed requirements. Each response at a minimum must meet solicitation requirement statement. The government is not requesting or accepting alternate quotes.

Title: Award Notice for NAVAIR Additive Manufacturing Capability
Awardee: General Electric Company West Chester OH 45011 USA
Description: Classification Code: 9999 – MISCELLANEOUS ITEMS NAICS Code: 333248 – All Other Industrial Machinery Manufacturing Title: NAVAIR Additive Manufacturing Capability Solicitation: SPE4AX26RAMP1 Contract Award Date: March 27, 2026 Description(s): A firm fixed-price delivery order was awarded to General Electric Company doing business as Colibrium Additive (CAGE 89WB2), supporting the Navy with (3) M Line laser powder bed fusion (LPBF) and (1) M2 Series 5 LBPF additive machines and multiple material data sets to include all Data Rights Assertions and Specifically Negotiated License rights. In addition to the machines, this effort also is procuring material characterization/data curves (via license), manufacturing process instructions (via license), and specifications (some licensed) to additively manufacture NAVAIR components. This effort includes (6) Material Property Data Programs (MPDP) which are the generation of physical and mechanical property data for a specific machine and material combination and include material characterization, development of parameter, consolidated material and process specifications, and establishing design allowables for the properties tested. This effort also included (1) Thin Wall Fatigue characterization to determine the fatigue life of an as-printed wall thickness that is significantly reduced from the baseline test bar size. The vendor is also responsible for supply chain management, logistics planning, forecasting production requirements, long lead time parts procurement, diminishing manufacturing sources and material shortage issues, shipping serviceable assets, and the potential to provide procurement/manufacture of component parts in the future. Vendor is also responsible for execution of a training plan to train manufacturing engineers, quality engineers, design engineers, material engineers, and machine operators to be able to repeatedly produce airworthy aviation components in various material-process combinations. All contract items were solicited on a sole source basis under the authority of FAR 6.302-1(a)(1). This order was issued against contract SPE4AX-23-D-9421 IAW FAR 6.302-1. Title: Load Handling Equipment Annual Inspections - 12 Upper Mississippi River Locks and Dams
PopStreetAddress: 12 Lock and Dam sites along Mississippi River from Minneapolis, MN to Guttenberg, IA
Awardee: SPRINGFIELD SOLUTIONS LLC 4435 DEPRIEST ST Roseburg OR 97471 USA
Description: PLEASE NOTE -- CONTRACT WAS COMBINED WITH SECOND REQUIREMENT, NOT SOLICITED ON SAM.GOV that was below micropurchase threshold -- Price reflects combined requirements.--- The U.S. Army Corps of Engineers - St. Paul District requires a contractor to have qualified personnel perform annual inspections of Load Handling Equipment at twelve (12) Lock and Dam sites located along the Upper Mississippi River between Minneapolis, MN and Guttenberg, IA. The 128 pieces of load handling equipment (LHE) to be inspected includes overhead cranes, jib cranes, chain hoists, cable hoists, gantry cranes, trolleys and similar equipment. These are all considered Overhead Cranes for contractual purposes. The contractor shall functionally test all hydraulic, mechanical, and electrical systems through full range of operation IAW EM 385-1-1. All hoists are to be functionally tested without load. Inspectors will verify safety devices for proper operation. Wire ropes shall be inspected IAW appropriate OSHA, ASME EM 385-1-1 and manufacturer recommendations. Inspectors shall submit inspection forms and certificate of inspection for each piece of equipment within ten days of final inspection. Inspections should be completed no later than 8 MAY 2026. AMENDMENT 0001 ADDED 16MAR2026 -- Amendment updates Inventory Spreadsheet, adding information on manufacturer, model number and serial number, when available, and updating the equipment based on last years inspection reports. Amendment extends solicitation response period by one day.AMENDMENT 0002 ADDED 16MAR2026 -- Amendment adds Q and A with responses to vendor questions received.

Description: FACTOR 1 TECHNICAL FACTOR 2 DELIVERY FACTOR 3 PRICE FACTOR 1 Technical Quoter shall provide a description of the supplies to be quoted. The supplies shall be in accordance with the schedules and attachments of the solicitation. The following information is required: Descriptive literature to include warranties, special features, and characteristics, etc. The Government will rate more favorably quotes that meet or exceed the requirements of the equipment and service. The Government will rate less favorably quotes that do not give enough of a description of the supplies/equipment for the Government to determine if the equipment and services meets the requirements in the statement of work. FACTOR 2 Delivery Time Ability to be flexible with final installation dates is required as installation is based on construction completion. Ability to provide any updates and deliver upon statement of work objectives in a timely manner should be clearly outlined in your response. FACTOR 3 Price The quote should contain the quoter s best terms from a price standpoint. The Government will evaluate quotes for award purposes by adding the total price for all contract line-items. Complete all highlighted columns for each contract line-item numbers (CLIN) listed in the attached spreadsheet titled Price Sheet in Section D of the solicitation. Vendor may also submit price quote on their own formatted document, but the Price Sheet SHALL be completed and included with quote submission. Price quote must be submitted separately from the other factor submissions. In accordance with FAR 8.405-2(d), the quotes will undergo a comparative evaluation to determine which quote is the best overall offer to the government in terms of high technical capability, while also providing a competitive price. Quotes will be evaluated by performing a direct comparison of one offer with another in a uniform manner to determine which quote provides the government with its needs, as identified in the RFQ. The government reserves the right to consider a response that offers more than the minimum and select that response if it provides a benefit to the government. The government has the discretion to accept other than the lowest priced offeror that provides additional benefits. The government reserves the right to select a response that provides benefit to the Government that exceeds the minimum but is not required to do so. Responses may exceed requirements. Each response at a minimum must meet solicitation requirement statement. The government is not requesting or accepting alternate quotes.

Title: Event Space - SMC Graduation (Class 76)
Description: The Mission and Installation Contracting Command – Fort Bliss is issuing this solicitation to identify parties having an interest in and the resources to support and provide venue space and services. The requirement is for a one-day service contract to provide a commercial venue and comprehensive event support services for the SMC Class 76 Graduation Ceremony on 11 June 2026 in El Paso, Texas. The contractor shall furnish all personnel, equipment, supplies, tools, materials, and services necessary to support the full execution of the graduation event. Required services include venue rental, staging, audiovisual production, lighting, projection capability, security operations, general labor, custodial support, medical standby, and associated equipment as specified in the PWS. The contractor shall ensure the facility accommodates 8,000–10,000 attendees, provides designated seating for graduates and VIPs, and meets all accessibility, safety, and operational requirements. All services must comply with applicable Federal, State of Texas, and local regulations, as well as OSHA and installation safety standards. The intention is to procure these services on a competitive basis.

Title: Operation & Maintenance of the South Bay Wastewater Treatment Plant
Department/Ind.Agency: INTERNATIONAL BOUNDARY AND WATER COMMISSION: US-MEXICO
Sub-Tier: INTERNATIONAL BOUNDARY AND WATER COMMISSION: US-MEXICO
Awardee: VEOLIA WATER WEST OPERATING SERVICES INC Boston MA 02110 USA
Description: (Fixed?Price, Not Competitively Awarded, Includes Transition Options) This contract action was not awarded competitively, but it is a fixed?price contract. In accordance with FAR 6.302?1, Only One Responsible Source, the U.S. Section of the International Boundary and Water Commission (USIBWC) determined in a formal Determination & Findings that only the incumbent, Veolia Water North America – West, LLC, could provide uninterrupted Operations & Maintenance (O&M) services at the South Bay International Wastewater Treatment Plant (SBIWTP) during the required acquisition corrective?action period. The plant is a 24/7 critical?infrastructure facility where any operational lapse would result in immediate environmental, public?health, regulatory, and treaty?compliance impacts. The prior competitive solicitation (No. 191BWC26R0001) was canceled due to material deficiencies in the PWS, ambiguous requirements, and a significant undisclosed organizational conflict of interest (OCI), requiring a full re?solicitation under a defensible competitive process. The Government requires the full contract period to properly complete the next competitive procurement to be awarded upon the conclusion of this action. The period of performance for this sole?source contract is April 1, 2026 through March 31, 2027, with an additional two?month option period (April 1, 2027 through May 31, 2027) to support transition to a newly?awarded competitive successor contract if required. These transition CLINs ensure continuity of operations and prevent degradation of mission?essential wastewater treatment services during contractor turnover. This limited?duration, sole?source, fixed?price bridge action is required solely to maintain uninterrupted operations while the Government prepares, competes, and awards the upcoming full and open long?term O&M contract.
